regions.name: Central Paris (1st-4th arrondissement)
regions.description: The historic heart — Louvre, Notre-Dame, Marais district, and the Île de la Cité

regions.name: Iconic Paris (7th-8th arrondissement)
regions.description: Home to the Eiffel Tower, Champs-Élysées, Arc de Triomphe, and Musée d'Orsay
regions.bestFor: Iconic landmarks, luxury shopping, Seine river walks

regions.name: Montmartre & Beyond (18th arrondissement)
regions.description: Bohemian hilltop village, Sacré-Cœur Basilica, artists' studios, and cabarets
regions.bestFor: Art, village atmosphere, panoramic views, nightlife

attractions.name: Eiffel Tower
attractions.description: Paris's most iconic landmark — stunning at night when it sparkles every hour on the hour

attractions.name: Louvre Museum
attractions.description: World's largest art museum with 35,000+ works including the Mona Lisa and Venus de Milo

attractions.name: Notre-Dame Cathedral
attractions.description: Magnificent Gothic cathedral on the Île de la Cité, currently under restoration after the 2019 fire

attractions.name: Musée d'Orsay
attractions.description: Stunning collection of Impressionist masterpieces (Monet, Renoir, Van Gogh) in a converted railway station

attractions.name: Arc de Triomphe
attractions.description: Napoleon's triumphal arch at the top of Champs-Élysées with panoramic city views from the top

attractions.name: Sacré-Cœur Basilica
attractions.description: White Romano-Byzantine basilica atop Montmartre hill with the best free panoramic view of Paris

attractions.name: Palace of Versailles
attractions.description: Magnificent royal palace 30 minutes from Paris — ornate Hall of Mirrors and vast formal gardens

attractions.name: Le Marais District
attractions.description: Paris's most fashionable historic quarter — medieval streets, galleries, Jewish quarter, and Place des Vosges

attractions.name: Centre Pompidou
attractions.description: Iconic inside-out building housing France's premier modern art museum with stunning rooftop views

attractions.name: Seine River Cruise
attractions.description: 1-hour Bateaux Mouches boat cruise past all major landmarks — magical at sunset or at night

pros: Lowest hotel prices of the year
cons: Some tourist sites have reduced hours

april.weather: Mild and variable, 8-17°C, frequent spring showers
pros: City looks beautiful with spring flowers
cons: Easter holidays bring crowds and closures

july.weather: Warm and sunny, 18-28°C, occasional heat waves
cons: Locals leave for holiday — some small restaurants close

culturalTips: Always greet with 'Bonjour' before asking anything — launching straight into English is considered rude
The French take dining seriously — budget 2 hours for a sit-down restaurant meal
Tipping is not obligatory but rounding up or leaving 5-10% is appreciated
Validate your Métro ticket before boarding — inspectors do check and fines are steep
Most museums are free on the first Sunday of each month — go early
Dress smartly — Parisians judge by appearance; avoid obvious tourist gear in upscale areas
Many smaller shops close for lunch from 12:30-2:30pm
La Bise (cheek kisses) is the standard greeting among people who know each other

budget.description: Lively local neighborhoods, excellent Métro links, budget hotels and hostels at $70-110/night

mid.description: Central, walkable, 3-4 star hotels, close to major attractions, $160-280/night

luxury.description: Palace hotels, Eiffel Tower views, butler service, $400-1000+/night
